params.name: Schmidt Camera
params.description:
On May 31, 1930 AD, German optician Bernhard Schmidt invented a new type of camera to observe the sky.
The Schmidt camera is an optical camera used to photograph the sky. Unlike regular cameras, its lens is a spherical mirror that can capture a wide-angle view of the sky without distortion. The invention of the Schmidt camera was a groundbreaking achievement that changed astronomy and optics in the twentieth century.
